title: Pellegrinia lobbii
taxonomy: Pellegrinia lobbii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Styracaceae, which is a group of evergreen trees and shrubs native to eastern Asia. It is a monotypic genus, meaning that it contains only one species. Pellegrinia lobbii is a rare and endangered tree species, primarily found in the tropical forests of southern China. The plant is often characterized by its distinctive, white, fragrant flowers and dark, glossy leaves. Pellegrinia lobbii is also known for its unique, resinous bark and its ability to tolerate a range of environmental conditions.
